findings.evidence: `UniformlyEllipticOn` stores side conditions independently of the domain at `UniformEllipticity.lean:377-382`; this file adds nonempty restrictions at lines 93, 107, 133, and 143 solely to recover those side conditions from one piece.
findings.fix: State the general patching lemmas with explicit side conditions, e.g. `hlam : 0 < lam` and `hLam : lam ≤ Lam`, and make the iff lemmas include `0 < lam ∧ lam ≤ Lam ∧ ∀ i, ...` (and similarly for `∀ i ∈ s, ...`); derive nonempty convenience lemmas if still useful.
findings.issue: `UniformlyEllipticOn.iUnion`, `biUnion`, and the corresponding iff lemmas require `[Nonempty ι]`/`s.Nonempty`, which is stronger than the natural domain-locality theorem for arbitrary indexed unions.

findings.evidence: The existing general theorem is `lemma mono_set ... (hΩ : Ω' ⊆ Ω) : UniformlyEllipticOn Ω' a lam Lam` at `UniformEllipticity.lean:439-442`; the new proofs at lines 67-74 are exactly `h.mono_set Set.inter_subset_left/right`.
findings.fix: Remove these duplicated intersection lemmas, or replace them with a genuinely more general restriction/congruence theorem if a new API is needed.
findings.issue: `inter_left` and `inter_right` are special-case restriction lemmas already covered by the general `UniformlyEllipticOn.mono_set`.

summary: The indexed-union API is stated only for nonempty index families even though the natural fixed-constant statement works for empty families by carrying the side conditions explicitly. The intersection lemmas also duplicate the already-general `mono_set` API as special cases.
